lines.text: We're {{ELAPSED}} in. The sun's coming up over Lake Mendota and the Capitol dome is catching the light like nothing happened. Like we didn't just spend the whole night in the dark together.
06:00 Madison. Temperature twenty-three. Clear skies. The sirens lift in sixty minutes. After that, the city wakes up.
Final tally: one-eighty-seven confirmed kills. Twelve below last year at this point. The neighborhoods are quiet now.
We made it through. That's the data point that matters.
This last one's for everyone still listening. For Darren. For the woman by the lake. For the people barricaded in the Co-op. The Bouncing Souls. 'Lean on me.'
You chose to stay home. That choice was never small.
This is PRGE. We'll see you next year. Stay safe when the sirens lift. Stay safe when the law comes back. And thank you for staying with us tonight.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide final: 187 kills. East Side held 18.2% of total. North Side remained quiet — 9 kills, lowest in a decade.
Capitol Square perimeter held through 06:00. Willy Street barricade successful — zero incursions post-22:30. State Street ruins contained after 01:00 coordination event.
Sunrise at 06:47. All-clear sirens lift at 07:00 sharp. Emergency services resume normal dispatch. MorningCare triage begins 07:30.
